sections.summary: Member States have the authority to establish rules governing administrative fines for public authorities and bodies within their jurisdiction. sections.title: Fines for non-compliance with supervisory authority orders
sections.summary: Non-compliance with supervisory authority orders, such as those for processing limitations or data flow suspensions, is subject to fines up to 20 million EUR or 4% of global annual turnover, whichever is higher. This applies to specific regulatory actions.

sections.title: Member States may establish rules for administrative fines on public authorities
sections.summary: Member States have the authority to establish rules governing administrative fines for public authorities and bodies within their jurisdiction. These fines must be imposed in accordance with national law and must be effective, proportionate, and dissuasive. The rules are subject to the corrective powers of supervisory authorities as outlined in Article 58(2).

sections.title: Supervisory authorities must follow procedural safeguards when exercising powers
sections.summary: Supervisory authorities must apply appropriate procedural safeguards when exercising powers under this Article. These safeguards must comply with Union and Member State law and include effective judicial remedies and due process. The section ensures that authorities follow fair procedures when enforcing penalties.
sections.dtia_summary: This section requires procedural safeguards for supervisory authorities, which may affect DTIA requirements for ensuring fair and transparent enforcement of penalties across borders.

requirements.text: Supervisory authorities must follow appropriate procedural safeguards
Procedural safeguards must comply with Union and Member State law
Safeguards must include effective judicial remedies and due process

sections.source_quote: The exercise by the supervisory authority of its powers under this Article shall be subject to appropriate procedural safeguards in accordance with Union and Member State law, including effective judicial remedy and due process.

sections.heading: Where the legal system of the Member State does not provide for administrative fines, this Article may be applied
sections.title: Member States may apply administrative fines where national law lacks such mechanisms
sections.summary: If a Member State's legal system does not provide for administrative fines, it must establish a mechanism where fines are initiated by supervisory authorities and imposed by national courts. The fines must be effective, proportionate, and dissuasive. Member States must notify the Commission of their adopted laws by 25 May 2018 and any subsequent amendments.
sections.dtia_summary: This section addresses the implementation of fines in jurisdictions without existing administrative penalty frameworks, which may impact DTIA requirements for ensuring consistent enforcement across borders.

requirements.text: Fines must be initiated by competent supervisory authority and imposed by competent national courts
